data.description: Design and ship a minimal inter-surface messaging primitive for c11. Filesystem-contract-first mailbox at $C11_STATE/mailboxes/ with a shared _outbox/, per-surface inboxes, and pluggable delivery handlers (stdin, silent, watch, potentially exec). XML-tag wire format for PTY injection. Topic discovery via hybrid advertised+emergent registry. CLI is a thin convenience wrapper; direct file writes are equivalent. See design doc at docs/c11-messaging-primitive-design.md.
data.title: Inter-agent messaging primitive: mailbox + pluggable delivery handlers

data.body: Design doc drafted and attached. Full-force review kicked off (clear claude + clear codex, both reading the doc cold with a structured review prompt). Reviews will land at /tmp/c11-messaging-review-claude.md and /tmp/c11-messaging-review-codex.md. In parallel, working through the three gating decisions (envelope schema, tag name, v1 handler set).

Full-force review complete. Both reviewers landed cohesive pushbacks; merged synthesis attached. Strongly-endorsed-by-both: drop 'PTY is the queue' framing, commit to at-least-once, promote sweep + async PTY writes + dispatch log to v1 mandatory, lock schema + parity test as drift enforcement. Disagreements flagged: (1) exec in v1 — Codex says no (privilege boundary), Claude says yes (simple + valuable); (2) topic fan-out in v1 — Claude says cut, Codex says keep; (3) --via-ref in v1. Waiting on operator decisions.

v1 scope locked after merged review:
- exec handler: DEFER to v1.1 (Codex was right — privilege boundary needs allowlists + env policy + timeouts before safe)
- topic subscribe/fan-out: KEEP in v1 (coordination is load-bearing; discovery registry/advertises cut to v1.1)
- --via-ref for oversized bodies: INCLUDE in v1 (safety valve for >4KB; pairs with hard-cap rejection)
- field naming: snake_case throughout envelope
- PTY tag name: <c11-msg> (both reviewers agreed)

Plus all consensus items from merged review become v1 mandatory: drop 'PTY is the queue' framing, at-least-once delivery documented with receiver-dedupe-by-id, async PTY writes with 500ms timeout, _dispatch.log NDJSON for observability, schema + parity test as drift enforcement, periodic sweep every 5s, stale .tmp GC at 5min, atomic move to _processing/ for dispatch idempotency.

Design doc rewritten to reflect merged review + locked v1 scope. Major changes: added v1-scope-at-a-glance box at top, added §5 'Delivery semantics' (at-least-once, dedupe-by-id), added §6 'Observability' (_dispatch.log), added _processing/ atomic-move marker to architecture, killed 'PTY is the queue' framing throughout (inbox is the queue, PTY is the channel), specified async PTY writes with 500ms timeout, locked envelope schema with body_ref support, cut topic discovery registry from v1, cut TOON from v1, kept topic subscribe+fan-out in v1, moved all deferred items into a clear v1.1+ table. Ready for Stage 2 prototype.

data.note: Both touch the per-surface manifest/metadata layer (CMUX-11). C11-13 extends pane metadata with mailbox fields (delivery, subscribe, retention, advertises); CMUX-37's WorkspaceApplyPlan will absorb these as structured fields when Blueprints/Snapshots land. C11-13 ships independently without blocking on CMUX-37; manifest field namespace chosen so Blueprint serialization composes cleanly.

data.body:
CMUX-37 (Blueprints + Snapshots) found and linked as related_to. Design doc updated with alignment decisions locked:
- Mailbox aliases = surface names from CMUX-11 nameable-panes metadata (no second identity layer)
- Per-workspace mailbox scoping: $C11_STATE/workspaces/<ws>/mailboxes/
- Messaging config lives in existing pane metadata as namespaced keys (mailbox.delivery, mailbox.subscribe, mailbox.retention_days) — NOT a parallel manifest.json file. Composes cleanly with CMUX-37's WorkspaceApplyPlan via SurfaceSpec.metadata.
- CLI root: c11 mailbox (matches c11 workspace/snapshot/tree noun pattern)
- Skill in main c11 SKILL.md, not peer skill
- Sender auth: trust v1 (same-user scope; real threat model needs remote/cross-user)
- body_ref allowlist: per-workspace blobs/ dir
- Private mode (PTY-free for secrets): out of scope v1
- Inbox cap: 1000, reject to _rejected/ with recipient_inbox_full
- Relationship: related_to CMUX-37, NOT depends_on. C11-13 ships on CMUX-11's metadata layer; CMUX-37 absorbs it naturally.

Ready to start Stage 2 (vertical-slice prototype) once operator says go.

Alignment addendum locked and attached. Shared doc at docs/c11-13-cmux-37-alignment.md. Same five locked conventions; C11-13 ships on existing CMUX-11 metadata path without waiting on WorkspaceApplyPlan, and runtime mailbox configure continues to work direct-to-metadata even after WorkspaceApplyPlan lands.

data.reason: PR #73 merged; marking review→done via operator

provenance.reason: PR #73 merged; marking review→done via operator

data.body: Stage 2 vertical slice merged via PR #73 (f13410b4, 2026-04-24). Shipped: envelope JSON schema + golden fixtures; MailboxLayout + atomic .tmp→.msg; fsevent watcher + 5s periodic sweep + _processing/ dispatch marker; envelope validator with 4 KB size cap + _rejected/ quarantine; stdin handler with 500ms async PTY writes + EIO/EPIPE handling; _dispatch.log NDJSON; surface-name resolver; c11 mailbox send/recv/trace/tail CLI; stale .tmp GC; tests_v2 parity test (CLI vs raw file write); skill section. Went through trident review + 10 P0 rework commits + cmux→c11 @testable rename. Topics/subscribe, watch/silent handlers, body_ref, mailbox configure CLI deferred to follow-up (see Stage 3 in plan note).

Renumbered from C11-13 on 2026-05-18 to resolve short_id collision with task_01KPY5Y0XPRE4A0ER310X630AK (Tab bar chrome). Prior commit references c83a4aa84 / f13410b4a / ccc362170 still mean this ticket (mailbox primitive).
